title: iCE40 DSP inference: Inferred multiply-add not working if parentheses omitted
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

1. [Test module to reproduce the issue](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/dan-rodrigues/2a4c6ab5fbf98357f4069ccf375a7f91/raw/30511b28e2258a0bde3abfe8ec6b4cd5fbdcb747/infer_mac_test.v).
2. `yosys -p "synth_ice40 -dsp" infer_mac_test.v`

## Expected behavior

`SB_MAC16` multiply-add is inferred. This can be seen if the commented block is used instead of the currently uncommented one, where the extra parentheses are added:

```
=== infer_mac_test ===

   Number of wires:                 16
   Number of wire bits:            151
   Number of public wires:          16
   Number of public wire bits:     151
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  2
     SB_MAC16                        2
```

## Actual behavior

Only the multiply is inferred. The add seems to be implemented using extra cells:

```
=== infer_mac_test ===

   Number of wires:                 20
   Number of wire bits:            239
   Number of public wires:          20
   Number of public wire bits:     239
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                 89
     SB_CARRY                       23
     SB_DFF                         40
     SB_LUT4                        24
     SB_MAC16                        2
```

## Version

Latest `master` at time this issue was written:

`Yosys 0.9+2406 (git sha1 584780d7, clang 11.0.0 -fPIC -Os)`
